title: aidevops Project Testing Configuration
description: Per-repo testing configuration for the aidevops framework. Place at .aidevops/testing.json in the project root. Created interactively by /testing-setup (t1660.1). Read by the full-loop runtime testing gate (t1660.7), verify-brief.sh (t1660.4), and task-complete-helper.sh (t1660.5).

$schema.description: JSON Schema reference for editor autocomplete and validation.

environment.description: Primary development environment type. Determines which commands and tooling are available for runtime testing. npm: Node.js/npm/yarn/pnpm projects (Next.js, React, Vue, etc.). docker: Docker Compose or containerised services. localwp: WordPress sites managed by LocalWP (Local by Flywheel). expo: React Native / Expo mobile apps. xcode: iOS/macOS native apps built with Xcode. tauri: Desktop apps built with Tauri (Rust + web frontend). binary: Compiled binaries (Go, Rust, C, etc.) run directly. manual: No automated dev environment — human-only testing.

dev_command.description: Command to start the development server or application. Run from the project root. The full-loop gate starts this before smoke checks. Examples: 'npm run dev', 'docker compose up -d', 'cargo run', 'expo start'. Leave empty if the dev environment is always running (e.g., LocalWP managed by the app).

dev_stop_command.description: Command to stop the development server after testing. Only needed if dev_command starts a foreground process that must be explicitly stopped. Examples: 'docker compose down'. Processes started with & are stopped via PID tracking.

test_command.description: Command to run the automated test suite. Used for unit-tested level verification. Run from the project root. Examples: 'npm test', 'pytest', 'cargo test', 'go test ./...', 'xcodebuild test'.

smoke_urls.description: URLs to check after starting the dev environment. The full-loop gate verifies each URL returns HTTP 200. Checked in order — first failure stops the smoke check. Use relative paths for consistency; the base URL is derived from dev_port or dev_host.

items.description: Absolute URL to smoke-check. Must be reachable from localhost.

dev_port.description: Primary port the dev server listens on. Used to construct smoke_urls when not explicitly set, and to check if the dev server is already running before starting it.

dev_host.description: Hostname for the dev environment. Defaults to 'localhost'. Use for LocalWP sites (e.g., 'mysite.local') or Docker services with custom hostnames.

startup_wait_seconds.description: Seconds to wait after running dev_command before checking smoke_urls. Increase for slow-starting environments (Docker builds, Xcode simulators, Expo bundler). Default: 5.

required_level.description: Minimum testing level required for all PRs in this project, regardless of diff risk classification. Overrides the full-loop gate's automatic risk-based escalation. Use 'runtime-verified' for production-critical projects where every PR must be manually verified. Use 'unit-tested' for projects with comprehensive test suites. Leave unset to use automatic risk-based escalation (recommended for most projects).

skip_runtime_for_paths.description: Glob patterns for file paths where runtime testing is always skipped, even if required_level is set. Use for documentation, config, and test files that cannot affect runtime behaviour. Patterns are matched against git diff --name-only output.

items.description: Glob pattern relative to project root.

environment_config.description: Environment-specific configuration. The active section is determined by the 'environment' field. Only the matching section is used; others are ignored.

package_manager.description: Package manager to use. Auto-detected from lockfile if not set.

install_command.description: Command to install dependencies before running tests. Skipped if node_modules exists and is fresh.

build_command.description: Command to build the project before starting the dev server. Optional — only needed if the dev server requires a prior build step.

env_file.description: Path to the environment file to load before running dev/test commands. Relative to project root.

docker.description: Configuration for Docker Compose or containerised projects.

compose_file.description: Path to the Docker Compose file. Relative to project root.

services.description: Specific services to start. Empty array starts all services.

health_check_service.description: Service name to poll for health before running smoke checks. Uses 'docker compose ps' to check status.

pull_before_start.description: Run 'docker compose pull' before starting services. Useful for CI but slow for local dev.

localwp.description: Configuration for WordPress sites managed by LocalWP (Local by Flywheel).

site_name.description: LocalWP site name as shown in the Local app. Used to start/stop the site via the Local CLI.

site_domain.description: Local domain for the site (e.g., 'mysite.local'). Used to construct smoke_urls if not explicitly set.

wp_cli_path.description: Path to WP-CLI within the LocalWP site container. Defaults to the LocalWP bundled WP-CLI.

theme_build_command.description: Command to build the active theme's assets before testing. Run from the theme directory.

theme_path.description: Path to the active theme directory, relative to the WordPress root (wp-content/themes/). Used with theme_build_command.

expo.description: Configuration for React Native / Expo mobile app projects.

platform.description: Target platform for testing. 'both' runs tests on both platforms.

simulator_device.description: iOS simulator device name for testing. Ignored for Android.

android_avd.description: Android Virtual Device (AVD) name for testing.

use_expo_go.description: Use Expo Go for testing (faster) vs. building a development client. Set to false for projects with native modules.

tunnel.description: Use Expo tunnel mode for testing on physical devices. Requires ngrok.

xcode.description: Configuration for iOS/macOS native apps built with Xcode.

scheme.description: Xcode scheme to build and test.

workspace.description: Path to the .xcworkspace file. Required for CocoaPods projects. Relative to project root.

project.description: Path to the .xcodeproj file. Used when workspace is not set. Relative to project root.

destination.description: Xcode build destination. Passed to xcodebuild -destination.

configuration.description: Build configuration. Use Debug for testing, Release for distribution.

derived_data_path.description: Custom DerivedData path. Useful for CI caching. Relative to project root.

tauri.description: Configuration for desktop apps built with Tauri (Rust + web frontend).

frontend_dev_command.description: Command to start the frontend dev server (Vite, webpack, etc.). Tauri's dev mode starts this automatically if configured in tauri.conf.json, but can be overridden here.

frontend_port.description: Port the frontend dev server listens on. Tauri default is 1420.

features.description: Cargo features to enable when building/testing.

target.description: Rust target triple for cross-compilation. Leave empty for native target.

binary.description: Configuration for compiled binary projects (Go, Rust, C/C++, etc.).

build_command.description: Command to compile the binary before running.

binary_path.description: Path to the compiled binary, relative to project root. Used to run the binary for smoke testing.

run_args.description: Arguments to pass when running the binary for smoke testing.

is_server.description: Whether the binary is a long-running server (true) or a CLI tool that exits (false). Servers are started in the background and smoke-checked via smoke_urls. CLI tools are run and their exit code is checked.

manual.description: Configuration for projects requiring human-only testing (no automated dev environment).

instructions.description: Human-readable instructions for manual testing. Shown to the agent when runtime testing is required but the environment is manual. Should describe how to start the app and what to verify.
examples: Start the app via Xcode, navigate to Settings > Account, verify the login flow works end-to-end.
Open the LocalWP site, activate the theme, and check the homepage renders correctly.

testing_contact.description: GitHub username or email of the person responsible for manual testing. The agent will mention this person in the PR when manual testing is required.

proof_log.description: Configuration for the testing proof log (t1660.5). Controls how test results are recorded in the task completion audit trail.

enabled.description: Whether to record testing evidence in the proof log when task-complete-helper.sh is called with --testing-level.

log_path.description: Path to the proof log file, relative to project root. JSONL format, one record per task completion.

include_screenshots.description: Whether to capture and store screenshots as part of runtime-verified evidence. Requires browser-qa-helper.sh (t1660.3).

notes.description: Free-text notes about the testing setup for this project. Shown to the agent when it reads this file. Use to document quirks, known issues, or manual steps required before testing.
examples: Requires NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in .env.local before running dev server.
Docker must be running before starting. First run takes ~2 minutes to pull images.
LocalWP site must be started manually in the Local app before running tests.

testing_level.description: Testing rigour level. self-assessed: code review only. unit-tested: automated test suite passes. runtime-verified: application started and behaviour confirmed.
